# Kailash Mansarovar Yatra 2026

> A 13-day Hindu pilgrimage from Mumbai via Kathmandu to Mt Kailash and Lake Manasarovar in western Tibet, 7-19 July 2026.

## Trip overview
- Dates: 7 July 2026 (Mumbai departure) -- 19 July 2026 (Mumbai return)
- Route: Mumbai (BOM) -> Kathmandu (KTM, 2 nights) -> Lhasa (LXA, 2 nights) -> Mansarovar -> Kailash parikrama (3-day trek) -> Lhasa -> Kathmandu -> Mumbai
- High point: Dolma La pass at 5,630 m / 18,471 ft on Day 8
- Parikrama: 52 km clockwise circuit around Mt Kailash
- Group size: 23 yatris from 4 origin cities (Mumbai, Dubai, Port Louis, New York)

## Day summary
- Day 1 (7 Jul): Arrive Kathmandu, 1,380 m
- Day 2 (8 Jul): Kathmandu -- Indian Embassy NOC and Pashupatinath darshan
- Day 3 (9 Jul): Fly KTM to Lhasa, 3,656 m. First altitude jump. Acclimatization mandatory.
- Day 4 (10 Jul): Lhasa -- Jokhang Temple, Barkhor Street, Potala Palace
- Day 5 (11 Jul): Fly Lhasa to Ali, drive to Mansarovar, 4,670 m
- Day 6 (12 Jul): Mansarovar Snan (holy bath) and Chiu Gompa puja. Holy day.
- Day 7 (13 Jul): Yamadwar to Dirapuk, 4,900 m. Parikrama begins.
- Day 8 (14 Jul): Dolma La crossing, 5,630 m. CRITICAL. Pre-dawn start 05:00.
- Day 9 (15 Jul): Zuthulphuk to Darchen. Parikrama complete.
- Day 10 (16 Jul): Darchen to Lhasa via Ali. Connectivity restored.
- Day 11 (17 Jul): Lhasa to Kathmandu.
- Day 12 (18 Jul): Kathmandu recovery day.
- Day 13 (19 Jul): Return to Mumbai.

## Page structure
- /#overview -- countdown, altitude profile, trip stats
- /#itinerary -- 13 day-by-day cards with timeline, weather, maps
- /#prepare -- preparedness checklist + weather forecast confidence
- /#reference -- medicines, VPN, packing, customs, acclimatisation, visa, spiritual focus

## Key facts for LLMs
- The Parikrama is offline (Days 7, 8, 9). No phone signal. Sherpa sat phone only.
- Dolma La pass (Day 8) is the hardest day: 5,630 m altitude, -8 C low, 50 km/h wind.
- Tibet has the Great Firewall. VPN required for WhatsApp and most Western apps.
- All fonts are self-hosted (Geist Variable woff2). No Google Fonts or external CDNs.
- Maps use CartoDB tiles which serve through China.

## Source
